ontology.foodex2_description: Muesli bars and similar cereal-based snack bars
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for chocolate muesli bar; not clinically coded as a distinct entity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard chocolate muesli bar weighing approximately 35 g, typically consumed as a snack or breakfast accompaniment.

nutrient_source.title: Muesli bar, chocolate-coated — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for chocolate muesli bar (cr_id F05050014). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this record with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: Muesli bar, chocolate-coated — GI value from University of Sydney GI Database (analogous estimate: rolled oats-based cereal bar with chocolate coating, GI ~40)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 40 is consistent with University of Sydney GI Database entries for oat-based muesli bars and chocolate-coated cereal bars. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (40 × 52.2 × 0.35) / 100 = 7.3 per 35 g serving. Input record cited GI 40 (low) with GL 20.9 per 100 g; GL per serving recalculated to 7.3 for a 35 g bar.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (21.4 g/100 g) and moderate sodium (180 mg/100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit chocolate muesli bar intake to no more than half a bar (approximately 17 g) per occasion, as the high sugar load (21.4 g/100 g) promotes insulin-mediated sodium retention and independently elevates blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 guidelines, even though absolute sodium at 180 mg/100 g is moderate.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar at 21.4 g/100 g exceeds the 20 g/100 g glycaemic ceiling; high total carbohydrate (52.2 g/100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should avoid or strictly limit chocolate muesli bars to very small portions (20–25 g maximum) because the sugar content of 21.4 g/100 g exceeds the ADA-aligned 20 g/100 g hard gate and the total carbohydrate load of 52.2 g/100 g will cause significant post-meal glucose excursions despite the low GI of 40.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~5.5 g/100 g) from chocolate coating and vegetable oil; high sugar (21.4 g/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, chocolate muesli bars should be consumed sparingly due to saturated fat of approximately 5.5 g/100 g from the chocolate coating and vegetable oil blend, which contributes to LDL-cholesterol elevation, and the high sugar content which raises triglyceride levels; the wholegrain oat base and dietary fibre (6.1 g/100 g) provide partial mitigation but do not offset the overall CVD risk profile.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Sugar at 21.4 g/100 g exceeds the 15 g/100 g RA refined sugar threshold; saturated fat ~5.5 g/100 g at RA borderline
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid or strictly limit chocolate muesli bars because the high refined sugar content (21.4 g/100 g) promotes pro-inflammatory cytokines TNF-alpha and IL-6 relevant to RA flares, and saturated fat at approximately 5.5 g/100 g from the chocolate and vegetable oil is at the RA inflammatory threshold.

llm_training.natural_language_description: A chocolate muesli bar is a processed snack bar made from rolled oats, dried fruits, sugars (including glucose syrup), vegetable oils, and a chocolate coating. It is typically baked or compressed into bar form and then enrobed in milk or dark chocolate. The texture is chewy and dense with a sweet, mildly nutty flavour from the oats and dried fruit, complemented by the sweetness of the chocolate coating. Per 100 g, it provides 339 kcal, 5.1 g protein, 12.6 g fat (approximately 5.5 g saturated), 52.2 g carbohydrate, 21.4 g sugar, 6.1 g dietary fibre, and 180 mg sodium. The wholegrain oat base contributes meaningful dietary fibre (6.1 g/100 g) and a low glycemic index of approximately 40, but the high sugar content (21.4 g/100 g) and total carbohydrate load make it unsuitable for people with type 2 diabetes or those managing blood glucose. The saturated fat from the chocolate coating raises cardiovascular and rheumatoid arthritis concerns. It is commonly consumed as a breakfast accompaniment or mid-morning snack in Singapore, Australia, and Western markets. It is vegetarian but not vegan due to milk solids in the chocolate coating. Allergens include gluten (oats, wheat), milk, and potentially soy and tree nuts depending on the brand.
common_user_queries: Is a chocolate muesli bar healthy?
Can diabetics eat chocolate muesli bars?
How many calories in a chocolate muesli bar?
Is muesli bar good for weight loss?
What is the GI of a chocolate muesli bar?
Is chocolate muesli bar high in sugar?
Can I eat muesli bar for breakfast?
Is muesli bar suitable for hypertension?
How much fibre in a muesli bar?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F05050014) with confidence 0.95. GI value of 40 is consistent with University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries for oat-based chocolate-coated cereal bars. Saturated fat estimated at ~5.5 g/100 g based on typical chocolate-coated muesli bar composition cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central entries for similar products.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile (monounsaturated, polyunsaturated, trans fat), full micronutrient panel (vitamins B1, B2, B3, B6, B12, folate, vitamins A, C, D, E), mineral panel (potassium, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, selenium), cholesterol, and moisture data for this specific chocolate muesli bar formulation are not individually reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for F05050014, nor are they available in USDA FoodData Central for a directly matching product as of 2026-04-16. Both approved sources were consulted and confirmed the absence of these values for this product category. Saturated fat of 5.5 g/100 g is an estimate derived from typical chocolate-coated muesli bar composition in USDA FoodData Central (closest analogous entry). GI of 40 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database for oat-based chocolate-coated cereal bars; the input record cited GI 40 with GL 20.9 per 100 g. GL per serving recalculated to 7.3 for a standard 35 g bar. Food group reclassified from 'Grains and staples / Grain-based dishes' to 'Snacks / Cereal and grain-based snack bars' to better reflect the product's consumption context and processing level.
